page: Amazon Web Services m7a.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro System. It features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs using the x86_64 architecture, driven by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieves top-tier status in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and demonstrates strong results in PassMark CPU and memory tests. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with strong read and write speeds for both small and large blocks. Cryptographic and vector processing capabilities are supported by hardware flags such as AVX-512 and AES. In LLM inference tests, the server delivers top-tier prompt processing speeds for small models and strong text generation performance for medium models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m7a.12xlarge offers high resource density and dedicated compute resources without hyperthreading overhead, making it a viable option for workloads requiring predictable CPU performance. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs positions this instance as a cost-effective choice for memory-balanced, non-accelerated workloads. It is well-suited for database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and medium-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m7a.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro System, it includes 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and strong memory bandwidth, supported by advanced instruction sets like AVX-512. It excels in database operations, software compilation, and static web serving, offering a balanced, high-density compute profile.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 192 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 19 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.

tagline: A dedicated 48-core AMD EPYC platform with DDR5 memory, engineered for high-throughput multi-core processing and balanced general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
192.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and strong memory bandwidth
19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
